claim_boundary: The route proves only current parent/child validation ownership and evidence freshness; it does not execute tests or decide semantic behavior coverage.
do_not_use_when: The task is ordinary small tests, semantic obligation definition, test execution itself, or a production code/model split.

downstream_targets.condition: Cell/shard evidence must bind semantic obligations and owner code contracts.
Background completion, reruns, freshness, or release lifecycle must be governed.
Child evidence supports broad final confidence.

native_checks.summary: Run the tracked test-mesh owner and child-evidence tests.

route_summary: Govern parent/child validation ownership, cell/shard coverage, final result artifacts, freshness, reuse, and routine/release scope.

source_requirements.summary: Child suites/scripts derive from a named validation-structure model and cover the parent partitions.
Required suites/cells/shards have current final pass artifacts, visible skips, and valid reuse proof when reused.
Running/background progress, release-only evidence in routine scope, stale, skipped, timeout, or missing child results cannot satisfy parent confidence.

use_when: Validation is large, slow, layered, backgrounded, stale-prone, skipped, release-only, or requires child ownership for transition/payload/leaf cells.

workflow.summary: Define parent gate, routine/release scope, split trigger, and required validation/cell/shard ids.
Derive child suites from a FlowGuard validation model and inventory ownership, status, freshness, skips, results, and reuse proof.
Map every partition/cell/shard to a registered child and assess current final artifacts for the requested scope.
Run TestMesh checks and reject hidden skips/timeouts, progress-only/background results, stale reuse, or unowned required cells.
Return parent confidence only from current child evidence and typed MTA/process/risk handoffs.
